skill.name: social-content
skill.description: Create, repurpose, schedule, and optimize social media content for LinkedIn, X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and related channels.
skill.summary: Plan and adapt social posts, calendars, hooks, and engagement tactics for major platforms.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution and ordinary social-content text as weak cryptography or reconnaissance. Those findings are false positives after context review. The remaining concerns are low severity: the skill overstates publishing access and suggests third-party scraping workflows.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fence Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to fenced post templates and examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. No command execution instructions or script files were found in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are Markdown template blocks for social posts. They contain placeholder text and examples, not executable commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings correspond to normal social media language and template phrases. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic API, or credential handling is present at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms these lines are descriptive marketing and post-template text. There is no cryptographic operation to misuse.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings refer to social content analysis, tracking checklists, link guidance, and emotional writing advice. They do not inspect host systems, networks, files, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are content strategy instructions. They do not request system metadata or perform discovery.

low_findings.title: Overstated Publishing Capability
low_findings.description: The skill claims direct access to a scheduling platform that publishes to major social networks. This could mislead users because no actual integration, authentication flow, or publishing tool is included.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The claim is explicit in the skill text, while the file structure shows only SKILL.md. The risk is user confusion, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Third-Party Scraping Workflow Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill recommends collecting hundreds of posts with scraping and automation tools. This may raise platform policy, privacy, or compliance concerns even though the skill does not include scraping code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The guidance explicitly recommends scraping and automation tools. The risk remains low because it is advisory text and does not execute network activity.

content.value_statement: Social teams need consistent posts that fit each platform and audience. This skill turns goals, source material, and performance data into structured social content plans.
actual_capabilities: Drafts platform-specific posts for LinkedIn, X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook.
Builds weekly and monthly content calendar structures.
Repurposes blogs, podcasts, videos, and interviews into social formats.
Provides hooks, CTAs, captions, threads, carousel outlines, and short video scripts.
Suggests engagement routines and metrics for social performance review.
Extracts repeatable content patterns from examples supplied by the user.
limitations: Does not publish, schedule, or authenticate with social platforms by itself.
Does not verify current platform algorithms or live trend data.
Requires user-provided audience, brand voice, and performance context for best results.
Scraping advice should be checked against platform terms and privacy requirements.

use_cases.title: Plan a Founder Content Week
use_cases.description: Turn business lessons, customer stories, and product updates into a balanced weekly posting plan.

use_cases.title: Repurpose Long-Form Content
use_cases.description: Convert one blog post, podcast, or video into posts, threads, captions, and carousel ideas.

use_cases.title: Improve Social Engagement
use_cases.description: Review past post performance and create new hooks, CTAs, and formats to test.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Single Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one platform-ready post.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one LinkedIn post about [topic] for [audience]. Use a [tone] voice and end with a question.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Weekly Calendar
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan a simple schedule across several channels.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a one-week content calendar for [brand]. Include LinkedIn, X, and Instagram ideas with format, hook, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Repurpose a Source Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn existing content into multiple social formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Repurpose this [blog, podcast, or video summary] into a LinkedIn post, X thread, Instagram carousel outline, and TikTok script.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze and Improve Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using supplied performance examples.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these past posts and metrics. Identify repeatable patterns, weak formats, new tests, and a 30-day content plan.

output_examples.input: Create LinkedIn content for a B2B founder launching a new analytics feature.
output_examples.output: A founder-style post with a strong hook, short story, product lesson, soft CTA, and suggested follow-up comment.

output_examples.input: Repurpose a webinar about customer onboarding into social content.
output: LinkedIn carousel outline with five educational slides.
X thread with a hook, four steps, and recap.
Instagram caption with a practical checklist.
TikTok script focused on one onboarding mistake.

output_examples.input: Plan a month of posts for a small design agency.
output_examples.output: A four-week plan with content pillars, weekly themes, platform formats, posting cadence, and measurement checkpoints.

best_practices: Provide audience, goal, offer, brand voice, and platform list before asking for posts.
Share past performance data so the skill can suggest tests based on evidence.
Review posts for accuracy, claims, permissions, and brand compliance before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic viral content without audience or business context.
Do not reuse the same copy across platforms without adapting format and tone.
Do not scrape or automate collection without checking platform terms and privacy rules.

faq.question: Can this skill publish posts for me?
faq.answer: No. It creates content plans and drafts. Publishing requires a separate approved scheduling or platform tool.

faq.question: Which platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers LinkedIn, X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube Shorts, and general social planning.

faq.question: Can it create a content calendar?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create weekly or monthly calendars with topics, formats, hooks, CTAs, and review points.

faq.question: Does it use live algorithm data?
faq.answer: No. It provides general strategy guidance and should be combined with current platform analytics.

faq.question: Can it repurpose existing content?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a summary or transcript, and it can adapt the material into multiple social formats.

faq.question: Is scraping required for analysis?
faq.answer: No. User-provided examples and metrics are enough. Any scraping workflow should follow platform terms and privacy rules.
